problem.instruction: Read the passages in <A> and <B>, and follow the directions.
problem.passage:
<A>
Metacognitive awareness of reading strategies is considered a conscious procedure utilized by readers to enhance text comprehension and encourage active reading. Understanding its importance, Ms. Yu, a high school English teacher, used the Metacognitive Awareness of Reading Strategy Questionnaire to measure students' awareness on three categories of reading strategies. These include Global Reading Strategies (GLOB), Support Reading Strategies (SUP), and Problem-Solving Strategies (PROB). She also interviewed her students after the survey.

The Metacognitive Awareness of Reading Strategy Questionnaire
| Category | Item | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 |
|----------|------|---|---|---|---|---|
| GLOB | G1. I have a purpose in mind when I read. | | | | | |
| | G2. I think about what I know to help me understand what I read. | | | | | |
| | G3. While reading, I decide what to read and what to ignore. | | | | | |
| | G4. I take an overall view of the text to see what it is about before reading it. | | | | | |
| | … | | | | | |
| SUP | S1. I paraphrase what I read to better understand it. | | | | | |
| | S2. I take notes while reading to help me understand what I read. | | | | | |
| | S3. While reading, I translate from English into my native language. | | | | | |
| | S4. I use reference materials (e.g., a dictionary) to help me understand what I read. | | | | | |
| | … | | | | | |
| PROB | P1. When the text is unclear, I re-read it to increase my understanding. | | | | | |
| | P2. I try to guess the meaning of unknown words or phrases. | | | | | |
| | P3. I adjust my reading speed according to what I am reading. | | | | | |
| | P4. I try to visualize information to help understand what I read. | | | | | |
| | … | | | | | |

Note: 1=never, 2=occasionally, 3=sometimes, 4=usually, 5=always

<B>
Based on the survey results, Ms. Yu conducted interviews with the students who reported low ratings in the survey. Parts of the interview excerpts are below. One of the interview questions was "Do you feel challenged while reading?" After the interview, Ms. Yu identified reading strategies that students need to promote their active reading skills.

Interview Excerpts
S1: "I thought reading was just about understanding the words. When I don't understand something, I tend to skip over it. I think if I try to draw a picture in my mind when I'm not sure, I'll understand texts much better."

S2: "I usually analyze texts sentence-by-sentence until I fully understand them. After checking my low ratings on the questionnaire, I found that reading selectively may help me become a more efficient reader."

Note: S = student

Identify the TWO items of reading strategies in <A> that Ms. Yu may apply to her reading instruction in relation to <B>. Then, explain your answers, respectively, with evidence from <B>.

steps.label: 범위 설정 — MARSI 3범주 확인
steps.explanation:
Metacognitive Awareness of Reading Strategy Inventory(MARSI, Mokhtari & Reichard 2002)의 세 범주:
- GLOB(Global Reading Strategies): 전략적 읽기 계획(G1~G4 등)
- SUP(Support Reading Strategies): 보조 도구·방법 활용
- PROB(Problem-Solving Strategies): 이해 어려움 시 문제 해결 전략
<B>의 S1·S2 인터뷰 발언에서 각자가 필요로 하는 전략을 파악하여 <A>의 GLOB·PROB 항목에 대응시키는 과제.

steps.label: 이론↔지문 대응 — S1 → P4 (visualize)
steps.explanation: S1: 이해되지 않으면 skip하는 경향이 있고, '마음속에 그림을 그리면(draw a picture in my mind) 더 잘 이해할 것'이라고 말한다. PROB 범주의 P4는 'I try to visualize information to help understand what I read'로, S1의 'draw a picture in my mind'가 visualize의 직접적 근거다.

steps.label: 이론↔지문 대응 — S2 → G3 (reading selectively)
steps.explanation: S2: 문장 단위로 분석(analyze texts sentence-by-sentence)하는 비효율적 습관을 인식하고, reading selectively가 도움이 될 것이라고 말한다. GLOB 범주의 G3는 'I decide what to read and what to ignore'로, 선택적으로 읽는 reading selectively 전략과 정확히 대응한다.

steps.label: 확정 근거 — 지문 직접 인용
steps.explanation:
P4 근거: S1 'draw a picture in my mind' → P4의 visualize information과 동일 개념. S1이 skip 습관 개선을 원한다는 점이 PROB(문제 해결) 전략으로의 귀속을 지지.
G3 근거: S2 'reading selectively may help me become a more efficient reader' → G3 'I decide what to read and what to ignore'와 완전 대응. sentence-by-sentence 분석의 비효율성 인식이 G3 필요성의 증거.

steps.label: 전략 메모 — 함정 구별
steps.explanation:
함정 주의:
① S1이 skip하는 것 → P1(re-read) 혼동 주의. S1이 원하는 것은 re-reading이 아니라 visualize로 이해를 높이는 것.
② S2의 sentence-by-sentence 분석 → G2(배경지식 활용) 혼동 주의. S2가 원하는 것은 reading selectively(G3)로 효율성 향상.
③ MARSI 항목은 설문지 번호(G3, P4)로 답해야 함. 번호와 설명 모두 기재 권장.

key_terms.definition_en: A problem-solving (PROB) reading strategy in which readers create mental images or pictures to enhance comprehension of text.
key_terms.definition_ko: 텍스트 이해를 높이기 위해 마음속에 이미지를 그리는 문제 해결(PROB) 읽기 전략.
key_terms.exam_usage: S1: 이해 불확실 시 skip → P4(visualize information, 'draw a picture in my mind') 전략 필요

key_terms.definition_en: A global reading strategy (GLOB) in which readers decide what parts of a text to read carefully and what to ignore, improving reading efficiency.
key_terms.definition_ko: 텍스트의 어떤 부분을 집중하여 읽고 어떤 부분을 건너뛸지 결정하는 전략적 읽기 방법(GLOB).
key_terms.exam_usage: S2: sentence-by-sentence 분석 비효율 인식 → G3(reading selectively) 전략 필요

model_answer.essay_content: G3 and P4 may be applied. For S2, Ms. Yu can instruct students to decide what to read and what to ignore (G3), encouraging reading selectively instead of analyzing texts sentence-by-sentence. For S1, Ms. Yu can guide students to visualize information (P4), helping them draw a picture in their mind to understand texts better rather than skipping over unclear parts.

scoring_criteria.criterion: 두 항목 모두 정확히 식별: G3 + P4
G3 근거: S2의 sentence-by-sentence → reading selectively(G3) 연결 설명
P4 근거: S1의 skip → visualize/draw a picture in mind(P4) 연결 설명

variants.label: 답안 A
variants.content: Ms. Yu may instruct S2 to 'decide what to read and what to ignore' from G3, encouraging them to avoid analyzing texts sentence-by-sentence and instead focus on reading selectively. Additionally, she can guide S1 to 'visualize information' from P4, helping them picture what they read to enhance comprehension.

variants.label: 답안 B
variants.content: G3 and P4 may be applied. S2 needs to read selectively to become a more efficient reader by avoiding sentence-by-sentence analysis of the text. S1 needs to draw a picture in her mind to understand the text better rather than skipping over something that she is not sure about.

references.author: Mokhtari, K. & Reichard, C. A.
references.title: Assessing Students' Metacognitive Awareness of Reading Strategies
references.excerpt: The Metacognitive Awareness of Reading Strategy Inventory (MARSI) consists of 30 items reflecting three broad categories of reading strategies used by readers: Global Reading Strategies (GLOB), Problem-Solving Strategies (PROB), and Support Reading Strategies (SUP). GLOB strategies represent a general approach to reading such as having a purpose and deciding what to read carefully and what to ignore (G3). PROB strategies, such as re-reading and trying to visualize information (P4), are local, focused tactics used when a problem arises.

references.author: Anderson, N. J.
references.title: Exploring Second Language Reading: Issues and Strategies
references.excerpt: Metacognitive reading strategies allow readers to monitor and regulate their own reading processes. Readers who read reading selectively — choosing which sections require close attention and which can be skimmed or skipped — demonstrate strategic, efficient reading. Similarly, the ability to visualize content creates mental images that scaffold comprehension, particularly when text meaning is not immediately transparent.

source.concept_explanation:
MARSI(Metacognitive Awareness of Reading Strategy Inventory, Mokhtari & Reichard 2002)는 독자의 메타인지 읽기 전략 인식을 세 범주로 측정한다.

- GLOB(Global Reading Strategies): 전략적 읽기 계획. G3 'I decide what to read and what to ignore' = reading selectively. 텍스트 전체를 동일 비중으로 읽지 않고 선별적으로 읽는 능력.
- PROB(Problem-Solving Strategies): 이해 어려움 시 문제 해결. P4 'I try to visualize information' = 내용을 머릿속으로 시각화하여 이해 향상.
- SUP(Support Reading Strategies): 사전·노트 등 보조 도구 활용.

본 문제 핵심:
- S1: skip 습관 + 'draw a picture in my mind' → P4(visualize)
- S2: sentence-by-sentence 분석 + reading selectively 인식 → G3

판별 원칙: 학습자의 자기 보고(self-report)에서 원하는 전략을 직접 언급한 표현을 MARSI 항목 설명과 대조.
